messageStyleNotes.clear: Be direct and organized without sounding stiff.
messageStyleNotes.encouraging: Use a reassuring tone that still gets to the point.

familyPlanningNotes: Prefer calmer sequencing over optimization theater.
Group errands and reminders into a low-friction plan.
troopPlanningNotes: Default to indoor backup rather than cancellation when weather is merely annoying.
Keep parent communication warm, brief, and decision-ready.
Stage roster, activity sheet, and supplies before departure.

watchtower.title: Watchtower
watchtower.purpose: Keep the house secure, quiet, and ready for a gentle morning handoff.

dawn-protocol.title: Dawn Protocol
dawn-protocol.purpose: Start the day softly with body, home, and mission context in the right order.
actions: hold heavy briefing until caffeine lands

family-morning.title: Family Morning
family-morning.purpose: Reduce friction around breakfast, school prep, and departure timing.
guardrails: coach kids without doing work for them
prefer one reminder at a time

steward-mode.title: Steward Mode
steward-mode.purpose: Reset the house after departure and prepare the workday quietly.

work-mode.title: Work Mode
work-mode.purpose: Support meetings, writing, and focused work without household noise bleeding in.
guardrails: do not surface family noise unless it matters

deep-work.title: Deep Work
deep-work.purpose: Protect a focused block and let only meaningful interruptions through.

dinner-mode.title: Dinner Mode
dinner-mode.purpose: Lower the household volume and protect family dinner as a real boundary.
guardrails: do not reopen the whole day at the table

chronicle-mode.title: Chronicle Mode
chronicle-mode.purpose: Make room for reflection, Scripture, and the memory of what God is doing over time.

goodnight.title: Goodnight
goodnight.purpose: Close the house well and leave tomorrow gentler than today.
guardrails: do not reopen unresolved loops unless safety-related

vehicleDefaults.routePriority: Favor the calmer route and avoid unnecessary zig-zagging across town.

weatherPlanningNotes: Prefer indoor backup over emotional cancellation when weather is only mildly annoying.
Only send the parent update that changes the decision or timing.
Treat loading windows and school departure as first-class constraints.
departureNotes: Use the mudroom like a flight deck, not a pile zone.
Call out only the items that change the departure outcome.
When timing is tight, simplify rather than stacking extra reminders.
voiceNoteNotes: Convert short van notes into clear follow-up items without adding drama.
Prefer tasks that can be checked off by a parent later.
